description: Palms cultivated primarily for landscape, garden, and indoor ornamental use. Includes parlour palms, areca/butterfly palm, bottle palm, foxtail palm, bangalow palm, bismarck palm, and many popular tropical garden genera.
meta_description: Complete taxonomy of ornamental palms: Chamaedorea, Dypsis, Rhapis, Bismarckia, Archontophoenix, Syagrus, Butia, Brahea and more.

genera.description: Elegant palms native to eastern Australia, widely planted in tropical and subtropical gardens worldwide for their graceful appearance. A. cunninghamiana (bangalow palm) is extremely common in Indian gardens.
Monotypic genus from Madagascar with massive silver-blue fan leaves. One of the most striking ornamental palms in large landscape projects.
Fan palms native to Mexico and Central America, adapted to dry and rocky habitats. Brahea armata (Mexican blue palm) with silver-blue leaves is a popular ornamental.

species.common_name: Mexican Blue Palm / Blue Hesper Palm

genera.description: South American palms producing edible jelly-like fruit. Butia capitata (jelly palm / wine palm) is popular in warm temperate and subtropical gardens worldwide.

species.common_name: Jelly Palm / Pindo Palm / Wine Palm

genera.description: Small to medium understory palms native to Central and South America. The most popular indoor palms, particularly C. elegans (parlour palm). 100+ species, most from cloud forests of Mexico and Guatemala.

Over 140 species endemic to Madagascar, including the hugely popular Dypsis lutescens (areca/butterfly palm) which is one of the world's most sold houseplants. Also includes the triangle palm D. decaryi.

species.common_name: Areca Palm / Butterfly Palm / Golden Cane Palm

genera.description: Five species of severely endangered palms endemic to the Mascarene Islands. Hyophorbe lagenicaulis (bottle palm) and H. verschaffeltii (spindle palm) are popular ornamental palms.
Monotypic genus from Chile. Jubaea chilensis is the world's most massive palm and was nearly harvested to extinction for its palm honey/wine syrup. Now protected.
Three species of large fan palms from the Mascarene Islands. Striking ornamentals with distinctive male and female plants (dioecious), each with different leaf coloration.
Monotypic genus endemic to Seychelles. Produces the world's heaviest seed (up to 25 kg). The double coconut or sea coconut is a national symbol of Seychelles.

species.common_name: Coco de Mer / Double Coconut / Sea Coconut

genera.description: Fan palms endemic to Hawaii and surrounding Pacific islands. Many species are critically endangered due to habitat loss and invasive predators eating seeds.

Clumping fan palms from East and Southeast Asia, prized for interior landscaping. Rhapis excelsa is one of the world's most popular indoor palms. Multiple cultivars exist, especially in Japanese collector circles.
Diverse genus of palms from South America. Syagrus romanzoffiana (queen palm) is widely planted as a street tree. Includes cocos palm relatives and licuri palm.
Tall fan palms native to the deserts of southwestern North America and northwestern Mexico. Widely planted as avenue trees worldwide, including in Indian cities.

species.common_name: California Fan Palm / Desert Fan Palm

genera.description: Monotypic genus native to Queensland, Australia. Wodyetia bifurcata is one of the most popular ornamental palms in tropical and subtropical regions worldwide, including India.
